tools.name: run_fast_slow_drain
tools.description: Run the Fast-Slow Drain (FSD) demo — Damiron-Nastasi 2008 oscillating tank. The canonical DRS-vs-DES event-count demonstration. Returns engine output including the event counts (DES vs DRS), tank-level trace, and cycle summary. ANTI-FABRICATION: numbers come from a real DRS engine run; quote verbati

tools.name: run_hamburger_duo
tools.description: Run the Hamburger Duo (HAM) demo — Andy Siprelle's 5-stage finite-source line, executed as both DES and DRS implementations on the same model so the event-count and throughput numbers can be compared apples-to-apples. Returns engine output for the side-by-side run. ANTI-FABRICATION: numbers come fro

tools.name: run_valdez_tanker
tools.description: Run the Valdez Tanker (VALD) demo — Koelling-Remy 1983 Alaska Pipeline model, the paradigm-integration motivator. Crude flows continuously into the Valdez Marine Terminal storage tank (Flow); tankers arrive discretely to drain it (Item); DRS handles both via F2I / I2F transitions. Returns engine out

tools.name: run_vegetable_plant
tools.description: Run the Vegetable Plant (VEG) demo — a Plant Builder distribution-control model. Two Making lines feed five Packing lines through eight surge bins; a DRS rate solver splits and rebalances the flow across the bins as the plant works through its campaign schedule. Returns the plant rollup (utilization

tools.name: run_chocolate_processing
tools.description: Run the Chocolate Processing (CHOC) demo — Plant Builder's joint DES↔DRS bridge. Three systems in series (Bean Processing → Cocoa Powder → Chocolate): DES schedules campaigns and injects equipment failures, a DRS rate solver carries the continuous flow, a bridge couples them. Exercises all 7 control

tools.name: run_sku_capacity
tools.description: Run the Bottling Line / SKU-capacity (SKU) demo — a sim-foundation parameter-set example. One 5-machine bottling line run for several products (SKUs as parameter sets). Returns, per SKU, OEE (identical ~55% — time-based interrupts) and indexed real output (swings >3x: 100 / 50 / 30 / 42) plus the pa

tools.name: run_tissue_line
tools.description: Run the Tissue Line (TIS) demo — a sim-foundation parameter-set example. One tissue line (Reel supply → Converter → Winder), three strategic decisions (each a parameter set): bypass converter / run converter / add storage tower. Returns per-decision throughput as % of nameplate (75.4 / 75.4 / 73.4),

tools.name: list_drs_demos
tools.description: List the seven DRS demos (Fast-Slow Drain · Hamburger Duo · Valdez Tanker · Vegetable Plant · Chocolate Processing · Bottling Line SKU capacity · Tissue Line). Each is reproducible against the engine via the run_* tools. Use this to discover what's available before calling describe_demo or a run_* t

tools.name: describe_demo
tools.description: Full per-demo write-up: history, what it teaches, what to expect from the run_* output. Use this to ground the user before triggering a sim run, or to explain WHY the demo exists when the user asks a conceptual question about it.

tools.name: explain_discrete_rate_simulation
tools.description: Return a textbook-tier explainer of Discrete Rate Simulation: how it differs from DES and CT, the three primitives (Constraint / Buffer / Interrupt), paradigm integration via F2I / I2F. Use this for 'what is DRS?' / 'how is this different from DES?' / 'where does DRS fit in the simulation landscape?

tools.name: explain_three_primitives
tools.description: Return a focused write-up of the three DRS modeling primitives: Constraint (rate-limiter), Buffer (accumulated state), Interrupt (stoppage). Use this when the user asks specifically about modeling primitives or how to spell a system in DRS. Deterministic text.

tools.name: explain_paradigm_integration
tools.description: Return an explainer of paradigm integration — how DRS handles systems with both flows and items via F2I (Flow-to-Item) and I2F (Item-to-Flow) primitives. Use this when the user asks about Valdez-Tanker-style mixed-paradigm systems or 'how do flows and items coexist'. Deterministic text.

tools.name: explain_des_vs_drs_event_complexity
tools.description: Return a focused write-up of the event-count complexity differences between DES and DRS, with the worked Fast-Slow Drain numbers (Continuous ~thousands vs DES ~500 vs DRS 10 events for the same 100-minute model). Use this when the user wants the practitioner-visible payoff of DRS — the 50× event-cou

tools.name: run_showcase
tools.description: LIVE EXPERIMENT — run a DRS demo against the real engine with parameters you choose, and get its verbatim run envelope (metadata, execution stats, metrics, details). This is the only tool that COMPUTES fresh output: pick a demo_id and dial its knobs (e.g. `stop_time` run length, or the MTBF/MTTR/goa

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "list_drs_demos" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: List the seven DRS demos (Fast-Slow Drain · Hamburger Duo · Valdez Tanker · Vegetable Plant · Chocolate Processing · Bottling Line SKU capacity · Tissue Line). …

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.
